name: El Castillo y Los Molinos de Consuegra – circular passing through Madridejos and Villafranca de los Caballeros
name_original: El Castillo y Los Molinos de Consuegra – circular pasando por Madridejos y Villafranca de los Caballeros
region_intro: Touring cycling around Consuegra offers routes through a diverse landscape where the steppe plains of La Mancha meet the low mountains of Montes de Toledo. The region features a plain at 700 meters above sea level to the northeast, characterized by agricultural areas. To the southwest, the Montes de Toledo present elevations between 1,200 and 1,400 meters, with holm oak forests and Mediterranean maquis. Cerro Calderico, a prominent hill, rises from the plains and is home to the town's iconic landmarks. Best touring cycling routes around Consuegra The most popular touring cycling route is Madridejos, Puerto Lápice, and Villafranca Lagoons Loop, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete, passing through agricultural landscapes and near lagoons. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is El Castillo y Los Molinos de Consuegra – circular passing through Madridejos and Villafranca de los Caballeros, a difficult 42.9 miles (69.0 km) path. This route features the historic castle and windmills of Consuegra, offering panoramic views of the La Mancha plains. Local touring cyclists also love the The castle and mills of Consuegra to Madridejos – circular passing through Villacañas, a 42.1 miles (67.8 km) trail leading through the plains of La Mancha and past the iconic windmills, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Consuegra is defined by the expansive steppe plains of La Mancha, the low Mediterranean mountains of Montes de Toledo, and the prominent Cerro Calderico hill with its historic windmills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides across the plains to more challenging routes with varied elevation. The routes in Consuegra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 170 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Consuegra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Consuegra?
A: There are over 45 touring cycling routes available around Consuegra. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate rides across the plains to more challenging routes with varied elevation.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Consuegra?
A: Consuegra offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You can expect to ride through the characteristic steppe plains of La Mancha, which are generally flat at around 700 meters above sea level, and also encounter the low Mediterranean mountains of Montes de Toledo to the southwest, featuring elevations between 1,200 and 1,400 meters. The prominent Cerro Calderico, home to the windmills and castle, also adds varied elevation to some routes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Consuegra?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Consuegra are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022588071/el-castillo-y-los-molinos-de-consuegra-circular-passing-through-madridejos-and-villafranca-de-los-caballeros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castillo y Los Molinos de Consuegra – circular passing through Madridejos and Villafranca de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt; route offers a challenging loop featuring the historic castle and windmills. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022701446/the-castle-and-mills-of-consuegra-to-madridejos-circular-passing-through-villacanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The castle and mills of Consuegra to Madridejos – circular passing through Villacañas&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the plains of La Mancha.

Q: Can I cycle to see the famous windmills and castle?
A: Absolutely. The iconic 16th-century windmills and the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2654903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Castle&lt;/a&gt; are perched atop &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Calderico&lt;/a&gt; and are central to many touring cycling routes. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra Windmills – Consuegra Castle loop from Consuegra&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to take you past these historic landmarks, offering panoramic views of the La Mancha landscape.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Consuegra?
A: Consuegra experiences a Mediterranean climate with around 329 sunny days annually. The best times for touring cycling are during the short autumns and springs, when most rainfall occurs and temperatures are milder. Summers can be quite hot, ranging from 22º to 39º C, while winters are cooler, from -5º to 12º C.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Consuegra?
A: While the guide features routes with moderate to difficult ratings, the area around the windmills and castle on Cerro Calderico offers easily accessible paths and shorter circular routes that can be suitable for families looking for scenic rides with historical points of interest. For longer rides, consider routes that stick to the flatter plains of La Mancha.

Q: What natural features can I discover while cycling in the region?
A: Beyond the iconic cultural landmarks, touring cyclists can explore diverse natural features. The region includes the 'Ardosa' steppe with its seasonal flora and wildlife like red partridges and great bustards. The Montes de Toledo offer holm oak forests and Mediterranean maquis. During rainy seasons, the Amarguillo riverbed also showcases flora and fauna typical of La Mancha's wetlands.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Consuegra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Consuegra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 4 reviews. Over 170 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Consuegra's varied terrain, often praising the unique combination of historical landmarks and the expansive, tranquil La Mancha plains.

Q: What other towns or attractions can I visit on a touring cycling trip from Consuegra?
A: Many touring cycling routes extend to nearby towns and attractions. You can cycle towards Madridejos and Villafranca de los Caballeros, visiting lagoons and other historical sites. The broader region also features other castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3792122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Negras Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2654920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Yébenes&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2661522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orgaz&lt;/a&gt;, offering opportunities to integrate cultural stops into your rides.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Consuegra offers several challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022588071/el-castillo-y-los-molinos-de-consuegra-circular-passing-through-madridejos-and-villafranca-de-los-caballeros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castillo y Los Molinos de Consuegra – circular passing through Madridejos and Villafranca de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 69.0 km path with significant elevation changes, providing a demanding ride through varied terrain.

Q: Where can I find viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: The most prominent viewpoints are found atop &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Calderico&lt;/a&gt;, where the Consuegra windmills and castle are located. From here, you get expansive, panoramic views of the La Mancha plains stretching out to the horizon. Many routes are designed to pass through or near this iconic hill, ensuring you experience these breathtaking vistas.

Q: Is public transport available to reach Consuegra for cycling?
A: Consuegra is accessible by public transport, typically by bus from larger cities like Toledo or Madrid. While specific information on bike carriage on these services can vary, it's advisable to check with the bus operators directly for their policies regarding bicycles before planning your trip.
tips: The fortress began to be built in the middle of the 10th century during the splendor era of the Caliphate of Córdoba, concluding its construction in the middle of the 13th century.

Symbol of Consuegra and La Mancha, the windmills crown the Calderico hill, drawing an absolutely unique picture. Universal thanks to Don Quixote, it is impossible to stand in front of them without imagining the adventures of the nobleman and visualizing an inspired Cervantes who, a good connoisseur of the area, wanted to capture them in his novel. As soon as we arrive we find the Bolero Mill, which is the Tourist Office.

This town is located next to an important lagoon area, of a brackish nature, at the confluence of the Cigüela and Amarguillo rivers. The lagoons have been used from the times of the Iron Age to the present, in the 18th century they were exploited to manufacture gunpowder. From the visit to this town we must highlight the church of the Asunción and among its hermitages that of Santo Cristo.
